title: Merope kunthiana
intro: Merope kunthiana, also known as 'Kapruva', is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and is known to offer various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Merope kunthiana is rich in bioactive constituents, including limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Merope kunthiana include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Merope kunthiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the genus Merope and is native to Central and South America.
